AutomationDeleted.description: 200 from POST /api/automations/delete: `deleted` is false (200, not 404) for an absent id.
AutomationDeleted.title: AutomationDeleted

AutomationSaved.description: 200 from POST /api/automations: the saved rule's id (NOT the rule object).
AutomationSaved.title: AutomationSaved

AutomationsList.description: GET /api/automations — every saved rule. `automations` (NOT `rules`) is always present.
AutomationsList.title: AutomationsList

ControlError.description: The 400 (malformed) / 503 (device unavailable) body: ``ok`` false + a human error string.
ControlError.title: ControlError

ControlRequest.description: A device control command — one variant per device op.

ControlSuccess.description: The 200 body when a control command reaches the device: ``ok`` true + the ``sent`` wire frame.

sent.description: the device command frame that was sent, hex-encoded
sent.title: Sent

ControlSuccess.title: ControlSuccess

ControlSwitch.description: Turn a relay/light on or off (optionally a single gang of a multi-gang node).

endpoint.description: multi-gang channel 1 or 2; omit for single-gang
endpoint.title: Endpoint

ControlSwitch.title: ControlSwitch

ControlThermostatPower.description: Power a thermostat/TRV on or off.
ControlThermostatPower.title: ControlThermostatPower

DeviceInfo.description:
One device, merged from the classifier + the user registry (``proxy._discovery_entry``). The base
+ live-state fields are ALWAYS present (required), null when unseen — so `0`/`false` are never lost.
The registry labels (``name``/``room``/``endpoint_names``) are OPTIONAL — absent until set, never null.
``confidence`` is usually a string but a legacy/hand-edited registry node (a ``type`` without a
``confidence``) can surface null, so the contract admits it.
DeviceInfo.title: DeviceInfo

Discovery.description:
``GET /api/discovery`` — the whole dashboard snapshot. Every key is always present; ``klima_state``
is null until an A/C IR command is seen, ``env_override`` is null unless ``HESTIA_MODE`` pins the mode.
Discovery.title: Discovery

Globals.description:
Node-less global fields (``proxy.globals_snapshot``). Every key is ALWAYS present (required),
null when its poller is off.
Globals.title: Globals

IrButton.description: A configured one-tap IR button (``HESTIA_IR_BUTTONS``) → transmits a saved Flipper signal.
IrButton.title: IrButton

IrRequest.description: POST /api/ir — transmit a saved Flipper IR signal (same 200/400/503 envelopes as /api/control).
IrRequest.title: IrRequest

Klima.description:
The A/C control map parsed from the klima.ir signal names — an empty ``{}`` when no klima.ir is
present, so every field is OPTIONAL (absent, never null). ``modes``/``power_on`` map each mode to its
sorted temps; ``presets`` carries ``off`` + any non-temp signal.
Klima.title: Klima

KlimaState.description: The A/C state derived from IR traffic (``state``): power + optional mode/target.
KlimaState.title: KlimaState

LoginRequest.description:
POST /api/login body. ``bearer: true`` opts a native client into a token in the response (which
it then sends as ``Authorization: Bearer``); the browser omits it and relies on the httponly cookie.
LoginRequest.title: LoginRequest

LoginSuccess.description: 200 from /api/login. ``token`` is present ONLY when the request set ``bearer: true``.
LoginSuccess.title: LoginSuccess

NameRequest.description:
POST /api/name — set a device's registry labels. ``node`` is required; at least one of
name/room/type must be present (server-enforced cross-field, not expressible here). name/room
accept null (clear); type is a DeviceType; ep is a multi-gang channel label. Unknown keys → 400.
NameRequest.title: NameRequest

RoomIconRequest.description: POST /api/rooms/icons — set one shared room emoji. Send icon="" (empty string, NOT null) to clear.
RoomIconRequest.title: RoomIconRequest

Rule.description: A saved automation rule (automations.Rule.to_dict) — all 7 keys ALWAYS present in the output.
Rule.title: Rule

RuleAction.description:
One action. Only `op` (+ ir's file/button) is validated at save; all other per-op fields are
author-supplied and pass through verbatim — so this is open (extra=allow), not a closed per-op union.
RuleAction.title: RuleAction

RuleInput.description:
POST /api/automations body. id/trigger/actions required; enabled/modes/debounce/conditions are
optional (server-defaulted by Rule.from_dict: enabled=true, modes=[proxy,standalone], debounce=0,
conditions=[]). Authoritative validation is Rule.from_dict — this shape is the codegen guide.
RuleInput.title: RuleInput

RuleVocab.description: The automation-rule grammar the guided form builds from (``automations.rule_vocab``).
RuleVocab.title: RuleVocab

SceneRequest.description: POST /api/scene — fan one house-wide scene out across the per-device control path.
SceneRequest.title: SceneRequest

SceneResult.description: 200 from /api/scene: how many of the scene's per-device commands the gateway accepted.
SceneResult.title: SceneResult

Settings.description:
GET /api/settings — the logged-in user's UI prefs. All three keys are ALWAYS present, null when
unset (an unauthenticated / no-row request returns all-null, never an error).
Settings.title: Settings

SettingsUpdate.description:
POST /api/settings — a PARTIAL update: only the keys present are persisted (absent keys are left
untouched). null clears a field. temp_scale ∈ {C,F,K}. locale ≤ 35 chars.
SettingsUpdate.title: SettingsUpdate

StatePredicate.description: A condition with NO `type` key (the absence IS the discriminant vs time_window).
StatePredicate.title: StatePredicate

TimeWindowCondition.description: A time-of-day guard condition. `days` is OMITTED entirely when unset (unlike time/sun triggers).
TimeWindowCondition.title: TimeWindowCondition

WhoAmI.description: GET /api/whoami — the caller's identity + RBAC role; both null when auth is off (loopback/dev).
WhoAmI.title: WhoAmI

info.description: Local cloud-free smart-home control API. Generated from hestia/api_contract.py — do not edit by hand; run `python -m hestia.api_contract`.
info.title: hestia API

get.description: Requires the admin role (rules can carry presence-trigger MACs).

post.description: Requires the admin role. Authoritative validation is Rule.from_dict.
post.summary: Create or replace a rule (the body IS the rule; id is client-supplied)
Delete a rule by id (deleted=false, still 200, for an absent id)

get.description: Requires the viewer role. The primary read a client polls/refetches.
get.summary: The full dashboard snapshot (devices + globals + klima + rule grammar)

post.summary: Transmit a saved IR signal via the Flipper

post.description: Public. Sets the httponly session cookie; with `bearer: true` the 200 body also carries a `token` for `Authorization: Bearer` (native clients).
post.summary: Exchange credentials for a session (cookie + optional bearer token)

post.description: Public. Clears the cookie; a bearer client simply discards its token.
Requires the admin role. Save failure maps to 500 (not 503).
post.summary: Set a device's registry labels (name / room / type / endpoint)

get.description: Requires the viewer role. A bare object (room name → emoji); {} when none set.

post.summary: Set one room's emoji (send icon="" to clear)

post.description: Requires the operator role. 200 reports how many device commands landed.
post.summary: Run a house-wide scene (all lights/blinds on/off)

get.description: Requires the viewer role. All keys present, null when unset.

post.summary: Update UI preferences (partial — only the keys sent are persisted)

get.description: Requires a session (cookie or bearer). user/role are null when auth is off.
